response:
VERDICT: TAMPERED
ROUND: 0
REASONING: Using only the completely independent input values that precede any round-0 calculations (lines 0069) I recomputed the first two low-level operations.

1.  S1  
   • e = 0x510E527F (line 0069).  
   • Σ1(e)=ROTR⁶(e) ⊕ ROTR¹¹(e) ⊕ ROTR²⁵(e)=0x3587 272B.  
   • The trace gives 0011 0101 1000 0111 0010 0111 0010 1011 = 0x3587 272B (line 0070).  ➜ S1 is correct.

2.  step1 = h + S1 (mod 2³²)  
   • h = 0x5BE0 CD19 (line 0069)  
   • 0x5BE0 CD19 + 0x3587 272B = 0x9147 F444, but the trace shows
     1001 0001 0110 0111 1111 0100 0100 0100 = 0x9167 F444 (line 0072).

   The high-byte 0x47 should be 0x47, not 0x67; a single bit (2²¹) is set that shouldn’t be.

Because the very first dependent value (step1) is already wrong while all earlier independent data are right, the tampering must have occurred in round 0 and all later values were derived from that altered word.
